fields.description:
## Why
Rec 1 + Rec 9 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risks R3/R4/R5/R8/R9). 14.9k LOC of bash with ~140 embedded python3 heredocs (27 in ticket-lib-api.sh, 20 in ticket-lib.sh) is the dominant maintenance liability for agent-driven development. The hard-won lessons live in the I1-I9 doctrine, the already-Python critical sections (ticket_txn.py, reducer, graph), and the 1,400+ test corpus — not in the bash itself. The regression-safe playbook is already proven in-repo: REBAR_NATIVE_READS (commits 7d53bc5 -> a93885e): port behind a kill-switch, pin parity, soak, remove the switch.

## What
Tiered strangler-fig port; each tier spawns its own child tickets when started; the relevant bash suite is the per-command characterization gate, run against BOTH impls until byte-identical before any default flips:
- Tier A: read shims (show/list/ready/search/deps) -> dispatcher calls _reads.py (story rich-glare-sake).
- Tier B: leaf writes (comment, tag/untag, set-file-impact, set-verify-commands, archive, scratch) -> rebar._commands; may temporarily shell to the bash write core.
- Tier C: compute-heavy bash (ticket-next-batch.sh 944 LOC, validate-issues.sh 945, ticket-list-epics.sh 449).
- Tier D: the write/sync core (_flock_stage_commit, _push_tickets_branch, _reconverge_tickets) LAST, behind REBAR_WRITE_CORE=bash|python, soaked one full release on this repo's own store.
- Tier E: delete the dispatcher; cli.py becomes a real argparse CLI over the same _commands functions as the library. Unpacked-disk constraint, jq prerequisite, and flock fallback all retire here.
Test consolidation rides along (Rec 9): each ported command's bash suite is translated to pytest and retired only after the default flips and soaks; parity tests are deleted as each duplication collapses.

## Scope
The full bash engine over multiple releases; one tier (and within tiers, one command) at a time. NOT in scope: any change to the store/locking design (ratified sound) or the event schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tier ordering + kill-switch discipline documented in docs/architecture.md and followed (default stays bash until parity green + dogfood soak)
- [ ] Each port ticket carries its bash suite as the characterization gate (byte-identical before flip)
- [ ] End state: one implementation + three facades; zero standalone shell test harness; no embedded heredocs
